source: Deep Hashing for Compact Binary Codes Learning.pdf

concepts.finding: Proposes a deep hashing framework that simultaneously learns feature representations and binary hash codes using pairwise loss and quantization regularization.
concepts.implication: Joint optimization of feature learning and hashing produces codes balancing discrimination and compactness, relevant for embedding compression in large-scale retrieval.
concepts.open_question: What is the minimum bit-width for query embeddings that preserves 95% of retrieval accuracy?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Quantized 32-bit query embeddings will preserve >97% of retrieval recall compared to 32-bit float embeddings while reducing memory by 4x.
